operation: First, initialize a CadQuery workplane aligned to the XY reference plane. Next, generate a solid cylinder with a height of 10 units and a radius of 5 units. Select the top (positive Z direction) face of the cylinder to create a new workplane on this surface, then offset the workplane origin 0 units along the X axis and -3 units along the Y axis. Draw a 6 unit long, 2 unit wide rectangle with centered positioning disabled, so its lower left corner sits at the offset origin. Finally, extrude this rectangle 1.5 units in the negative Z direction, creating a rectangular protrusion that extends out from the upper side edge of the cylinder.
geometry: The model is primarily a vertical right circular cylinder, with a small rectangular prism tab attached to the upper outer circumference of the cylinder. The tab extends radially outward from the cylinder side, sits perfectly flush with the top surface of the cylinder, and has a consistent vertical thickness matching its extrusion depth.
function: This is a cylindrical mechanical component with an integrated alignment tab, intended for use as a locating pin, mounting insert, or assembly stop, where the protruding tab ensures fixed rotational positioning when mated with a corresponding slotted part.
